title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard framework that enables AI agents and applications to communicate with external tools, APIs, databases, and services. The protocol uses JSON-RPC for client-server communication and supports multiple transport mechanisms including STDIO and HTTP, allowing seamless integration across AI frameworks like LangGraph, CrewAI, and Claude.
writeup: The video explains the Model Context Protocol (MCP), a standard framework that lets AI agents and applications communicate with external tools, APIs, databases, and services. It covers the protocol's architecture, including the MCP client and MCP server, and the use of JSON-RPC for client-server communication. It also describes the supported transport mechanisms, STDIO and HTTP, and how MCP integrates across AI frameworks including LangGraph, CrewAI, and Claude. The takeaway is a structured view of how MCP standardizes connections between AI systems and outside resources.
